text:
BOTTOM LINE — Tech-led bounce, breadth weak, semis dominated. Semiconductors surged 2.58%, reversing the prior day’s Nasdaq rout, but equal-weight and small caps lagged, signalling a narrow, high-conviction chase into AI hardware rather than broad recovery.

INDICES — VIX 16.5, down 0.5pts. Rank by return: SOXX +2.58%, QQQ +1.12%, SPY +0.36%, IWM +0.35%, DIA +0.04%. Small < large by 0.77pp. Gold rallied 1.37% as the dollar was flat.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 1.12, QQQ vol_ratio 1.35. Elevated volume on QQQ with price up = accumulation in tech. SPY volume slightly above average, confirming the move is not broad-based.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLK Technology +1.29%, XLE Energy +0.37%, XLF Financials +0.20%, XLB Materials +0.12%, XLI Industrials +0.04%, XLU Utilities -0.07%, XLY Consumer Disc -0.12%, XLC Comms -0.13%, XLRE Real Estate -0.49%, XLP Staples -1.38%, XLV Healthcare -1.93%. Top industries: DRAM +6.86%, IBIT +3.86%. Bottom: IHI -4.13%, JETS -1.19%. Money flowing into semiconductors and crypto, out of staples and healthcare. Rotation state: AI hardware leading.

POSITIONING — QQQ put-call ratio 1.34, elevated protection demand. SPY skew positive at +5.8%, indicating put premium. VIX at 16.5 is below its 20-day MA of 17.0, showing complacency. GEX flip for QQQ at 737.0, spot at 754 implies dealers are long gamma, dampening downside.

CROSS-ASSET — Gold +1.37% rose despite a flat dollar, likely a safe-haven bid from tech uncertainty. No major divergence in bonds or oil.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 47.4 Neutral. Top movers: DRAM +6.86% (semis), IBIT +3.86% (crypto), CIBR +3.15% (cybersecurity). Best momentum: MTUM bull, worst: SIZE bear. No vol spike tickers. Watch: SOXX today +2.58% — semiconductor bounce strength worth confirming; XLV -1.93% — breakdown in healthcare; IHI -4.13% — distribution in medtech.
